skill.name: technical-analysis
skill.description: Analyze APIs, data models, integrations, security requirements, compliance controls, and infrastructure documentation for software systems.
skill.summary: Create structured technical analysis for system requirements and architecture documentation.
skill.author: DoubleslashSE

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak crypto, and SAM-related alerts, but review found these are markdown templates and architecture diagrams, not executable code. The only practical concern is that users may place real credential locations or sensitive architecture details into generated documentation.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are fenced markdown, GraphQL, and diagram blocks. No evidence found of executable shell commands or instructions to run untrusted commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside documentation fences and architecture diagrams. The skill front matter allows Read, Grep, Glob, and LSP only, with no shell execution tool.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Windows SAM Alerts Are Lexical False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe SAML, OAuth2, bcrypt, AES-256, and placeholder security requirements. No evidence found of weak cryptographic implementation or Windows SAM database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear in user-facing security documentation examples, not code paths. SAML appears to have triggered SAM matching, and AES-256 and bcrypt are presented as acceptable controls.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Documentation Fields Need Care
low_findings.description: Templates ask users to document credential locations, sensitive data inventories, and network details. This is legitimate for technical analysis but could expose secrets if users enter actual credentials or unrestricted internal details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content legitimately requests security and infrastructure documentation fields. The risk depends on whether users include secret values or sensitive internal identifiers.

content.value_statement: Technical requirements are often scattered across APIs, schemas, integrations, and security controls. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured templates for complete technical analysis.
actual_capabilities: Documents REST, GraphQL, and event API structures with request, response, and business rule sections.
Creates entity, data flow, database schema, and relationship analysis templates.
Compares synchronous, asynchronous, file-based, and database integration patterns.
Organizes authentication, authorization, data protection, and compliance requirement analysis.
Structures infrastructure notes for environments, compute, networking, storage, and monitoring.
limitations: It provides analysis frameworks and templates, not automated vulnerability scanning.
It depends on user-provided system context and repository access.
It does not execute code, deploy systems, or validate live infrastructure.
It should not store raw secrets, credentials, or unrestricted internal network maps.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Requirements
use_cases.description: Create a consistent API inventory with endpoints, schemas, authentication, errors, and business rules.

use_cases.title: Assess Integration Designs
use_cases.description: Compare messaging, API, file transfer, and shared database options before documenting requirements.

use_cases.title: Review Security Documentation
use_cases.description: Structure authentication, authorization, data protection, compliance, and audit requirements for stakeholder review.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize System Interfaces
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of known APIs and events.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the technical-analysis skill to summarize the APIs and events in this system. Include purpose, authentication, payloads, responses, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Document Data Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate analysis of entities and database structures.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the data model for this feature. Document entities, key attributes, relationships, constraints, indexes, data flows, and unresolved requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Integration Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced design choice across multiple systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate integration options for these systems. Compare request-response, messaging, file transfer, and shared database patterns against latency, coupling, reliability, and operations.

prompt_templates.title: Create Technical Requirements Pack
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced end-to-end documentation for delivery planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a technical requirements pack covering APIs, data models, integrations, security controls, compliance needs, infrastructure dependencies, risks, and decisions.

output_examples.input: Analyze a customer orders API for implementation readiness.
output: Endpoint inventory with methods, paths, authentication, request fields, and response states.
Business rules for order creation, status changes, validation, and error handling.
Open questions about pagination, idempotency, rate limits, and audit logging.

output_examples.input: Compare integration options between a CRM and billing platform.
output: Pattern comparison for direct API calls, publish-subscribe events, batch files, and shared data access.
Recommendation based on latency, ownership, retry behavior, monitoring, and failure isolation.
Requirement checklist for authentication, data mapping, reconciliation, and service levels.

output_examples.input: Review security requirements for a regulated application.
output: Structured sections for authentication, authorization, data protection, compliance, and audit evidence.
Risks related to secrets handling, access reviews, retention, and logging completeness.
Questions for identity providers, data owners, security teams, and auditors.

best_practices: Provide architecture context, known constraints, and stakeholder goals before asking for analysis.
Use placeholders for credentials and internal identifiers instead of real secrets.
Review generated requirements with engineering, security, and product owners before delivery.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the output as a completed security assessment or penetration test.
Do not paste production secrets, tokens, private keys, or unrestricted network details.
Do not use generic templates without adapting them to the actual system behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or inspect live systems?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation and analysis skill that uses reading and search tools.

faq.question: Can it create API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides structures for REST, GraphQL, event APIs, schemas, responses, errors, and business rules.

faq.question: Can it choose an integration pattern?
faq.answer: It can compare patterns and document tradeoffs. Final architecture decisions still need human review.

faq.question: Is it a security scanner?
faq.answer: No. It helps document security requirements but does not test vulnerabilities or verify controls.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide system goals, interfaces, data models, constraints, existing documentation, and known risks.

faq.question: How should sensitive information be handled?
faq.answer: Use placeholders for secrets and restrict internal details to the minimum needed for analysis.
